headline: Africa Secures $900M to Bring Clean Cooking to Millions
meta_description: African nations secure $900 million in new funding to expand access to clean cooking technologies, improving health and lives across the continent.
summary_short: Nearly one billion Africans cook over charcoal and firewood daily. A major new financial commitment aims to change that and reduce health risks.
body:
Nearly one billion people across Africa depend on charcoal and firewood for cooking, exposing themselves and their families to serious indoor air pollution and health hazards. A major step forward came recently when African countries secured $900 million in fresh financial commitments to expand access to cleaner cooking technologies.

This funding represents meaningful progress toward a larger goal: the $3.1 billion investment deemed necessary to transform cooking practices across the continent. The growing commitments signal that governments, donors, and partners recognize both the human cost of relying on traditional fuels and the real opportunity to change the situation.

Access to clean cooking is transformative—it improves respiratory health, reduces the burden on families (especially women and children who spend the most time near cooking fires), and supports environmental sustainability. These new resources bring that possibility closer to reality for millions of people.
